title: KeeWeb
description: Free, cross-platform web password manager compatible with KeePass; opens and edits .kdbx files entirely in the browser.
installation: KeeWeb is a stateless, fully client-side password manager: the nginx container only serves static HTML/JS, and all cryptography (KeePass .kdbx parsing, AES/Argon2 key derivation) happens in the browser, so the server never sees your master password or plaintext. Because of this there is no database add-on and no server-side persistent volume - users open and save their .kdbx files from their own device or from an external storage provider they connect at runtime (WebDAV, Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, or generic file download/upload). To enable a storage provider you register an OAuth/app credential with that provider and configure it client-side; nothing needs to be seeded on the server. The official image listens on HTTP port 80 (and can self-sign TLS on 443) - here TLS is terminated at the Kubero ingress, so only port 80 is exposed. No SMTP, S3 or search engine is required. To self-host your own .kdbx over WebDAV, deploy a separate WebDAV server and point KeeWeb at its URL from the UI.
